mission.title: Install and Configure Spiffe on Kubernetes
mission.description: The SPIFFE Project provides a framework for securely identifying and authenticating services in a microservices architecture. Installing SPIFFE allows you to implement secure service-to-service communication using SPIFFE IDs and SVIDs.

steps.title: Add the SPIRE Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the SPIRE Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration to access the SPIRE installation package.
```bash
helm repo add spire https://spiffe.io/spire/helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install SPIRE using Helm
steps.description:
Install the SPIRE server and agent using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called 'spire' and deploy SPIRE into it.
```bash
helm install spire spire/spire --namespace spire --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify SPIRE installation
steps.description:
Check that the SPIRE server and agent pods are running successfully in the 'spire' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spire
```

steps.title: Configure SPIRE with resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the SPIRE server and agent to ensure they run efficiently within your cluster. Edit the values.yaml file and set the limits as needed, then upgrade the release.
```bash
helm upgrade spire spire/spire --namespace spire -f values.yaml
```

steps.title: Set up SPIFFE Trust Domain
steps.description:
Configure your SPIFFE Trust Domain by editing the SPIRE server configuration. You can do this by modifying the ConfigMap that contains the SPIRE server configuration.
```bash
kubectl edit configmap spire-server-config -n spire
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the SPIRE server and agent pods running in the 'spire' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and set up your SPIFFE Trust Domain.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add spire https://spiffe.io/spire/helm
helm repo update
helm upgrade spire spire/spire --namespace spire -f values.yaml

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the SPIRE release using Helm to remove the deployed resources from the cluster.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with SPIRE.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the 'spire' namespace and verify that all resources have been removed.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Create a backup of the current SPIRE release to ensure you can restore it if needed.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Helm repository and run the upgrade command to apply the latest version of SPIRE.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check that the SPIRE server and agent pods are running the new version successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue occurs when the SPIRE server or agent pods fail to start. Check the pod logs for errors using the following command:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: SPIRE server not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the SPIRE server is not responding, verify that it is running and check its logs for any errors:
```bash
kubectl logs spire-server-<pod-id> --namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration changes not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If changes to the SPIRE configuration are not taking effect, ensure that you have correctly edited the ConfigMap and restarted the SPIRE server:
```bash
kubectl delete pod spire-server-<pod-id> --namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade command fails, check for any version mismatches or required values in your values.yaml file. You can view the current release status with:
```bash
helm status spire --namespace spire
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have installed Helm and kubectl on your local machine.
